1.description: Interest rates This figure plots the 12-month maturity interest rates. The option-implied rate is based on put-call parity pairs of S&P 500 index options. The zero curve rate is from OptionMetrics. The constant maturity Treasury rate is from the H.15 filing of the St Louis Federal Reserve Bank. All interest rates are continuously compounded and expressed as a percentage. The period is from January 1996 through December 2022.

2.description: Cumulative returns Panel A plots the cumulative returns for a hypothetical one dollar investment in the dividend strip and the market. Panel B plots the cumulative excess returns of the strip in excess of the risk-free rate and the market in excess of the risk-free rate. Risk-free rate (rf) is the 1-month Treasury-bill rate. Panel C plots the cumulative excess returns of the strip in excess of the 2-year Treasury return and the market in excess of the 10-year Treasury return. The period is from January 1996 through December 2022.

3.description: Annualized standard deviation across different holding periods This figure plots the annualized standard deviation for excess market and strip returns for holding periods of 1, 6, 12, 18, 24, 30, and 36 months. The returns are in excess of the risk-free rate (panel A) and in excess of the Treasury returns (panel B). The period is from January 1996 through December 2022.

4.description: Annualized Sharpe ratio across different holding periods This figure plots the annualized Sharpe ratio for excess market and strip returns for holding periods of 1, 6, 12, 18, 24, 30, and 36 months. The returns are in excess of the risk-free rate (panel A) and in excess of the Treasury returns (panel B). The period is from January 1996 through December 2022.

5.description: Annualized Sharpe ratios: Subsamples This figure plots the annualized Sharpe ratio for holding periods of 1, 6, 12, 18, 24, 30, and 36 months. The returns are in excess of the risk-free rate (panel A) and in excess of the Treasury returns (panel B). The period is from January 1996 through October 2009 (left) and from December 2004 through December 2022 (right).
